mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an economic slowdown that compresses corporate revenue growth expectations, triggering a negative repricing of equity risk premiums and depressing S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicates robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in forward 60-day returns during identified housing recovery periods, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows zero statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding its historical trend level reflects capacity utilization peaks that precede margin compression and earnings downgrades, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 112, or the signal exhibits no incremental predictive power over a simple momentum baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive GDP growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which elevates the equity risk premium and compresses forward valuation multiples, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the inflation spike, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in forward 60-day returns during identified labor market normalization periods, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows zero statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained collapse in housing starts signals a contraction in residential investment and wealth effects, which drags down aggregate demand and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where HOUST falls below 1100, or the signal exhibits no incremental predictive power over a simple momentum baseline.
